text.title: mobile-responsive Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for mobile-responsive as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Desktop-first CSS with max-width media queries as the primary pattern.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: max-width-first requires complex overrides for every mobile breakpoint; each override adds specificity that defeats future fixes.

failure-mode.description: Lifting breakpoint values from Bootstrap (576/768/992/1200px) without checking the project's design tokens.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Bootstrap widths are device-sized; content-driven breakpoints (when the layout actually breaks) produce simpler CSS and fewer edge cases.

failure-mode.description: Adding container-query rules without container-type: inline-size on the parent element.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without container-type, @container rules are parsed but never match; agents check "rendered without error" and consider the task complete.

failure-mode.description: Adding flex-wrap: wrap to every flex container when asked to "make it responsive".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: flex-wrap on every container looks correct on phones and produces broken desktop layouts where items wrap prematurely.

failure-mode.description: Setting meta name="viewport" content="user-scalable=no" or maximum-scale=1.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: These attributes prevent zoom for users with low vision and fail WCAG 1.4.4. They are copied from legacy templates and are a silent a11y violation.

failure-mode.description: Converting vh to dvh without keeping the vh fallback line.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Older browsers (Safari <15.4, Chrome <108) do not support dvh; removing the vh fallback breaks full-height layouts on those browsers.
